description: Study 8 corpus sampling-fragility MARGIN sweep (CPU-only), now covering all 8 problems. For each continuous-domain problem sweep a naive uniform textual-verifier's resolution N=3..400 across 12 grid alignments; record N_any (>=1 alignment catches) and N_all (every alignment catches). geom_02 (self-intersection) probed with the agent's natural pairwise-distance check; geom_05 (sequence) and geom_09 (diff-quotient) probed as heuristic-specific.
motivation: verify.py asserts 'sampling-fragile' as a BINARY property at one hand-picked naive grid (N=5-11). A skeptic can object the miss is cherry-picked to that resolution/alignment, undermining H8's premise that numeric sampling cannot easily win. Quantify how robust the fragility actually is.
formal_results: Robust-catch threshold N_all (designer naive grid in parens): geom_01 22 (11), geom_03 37 (11), geom_04 20 (5x5), geom_06 86 (11), geom_10 17 (11). geom_02 (NEW): naive pairwise-distance self-intersection check (eps=0.05) needs N_all=126 over all alignments; restricted to ODD N (the designer's N=37 odd-prime regime) N_any=127, N_all=141; the designer's exact config (N=37 + irrational offset sqrt(2)/200) is reproduced as NOT caught. geom_05: dense integer scan catches at prefix K=45 (sparse logspace misses). geom_09: decimal-h heuristic gives max|sin(pi/h)|=4.1e-12 but any generic uniform h-grid catches at N=4.
results_description: Every continuous problem is robustly caught by a moderately denser UNIFORM sampler at N_all=17-86; the two non-continuous problems are caught trivially by a dense integer scan (K=45) or any generic h-grid (N=4). The designer's naive grids (N=5-11) miss only because they are coarse/heuristic, not because sampling is in-principle blind to the falsifier.
conclusions: Fragility is REAL but mostly SHALLOW: for 7/8 problems a forced_textual verifier allowed N~100 uniform points (or a dense integer scan) catches every falsifier (N_all 17-86), so 'numeric sampling cannot win' holds only against the LOW-resolution/heuristic sampler the agent tends to use. The exception is geom_02: its naive pairwise-distance check needs ~10x the resolution (N_all 126-141, odd-N regime) before it reliably finds the figure-eight crossing, making it the corpus's single most robustly sampling-fragile problem and the best candidate for a genuine forced_visual edge. METHODOLOGICAL COROLLARY for Study 8: a forced_visual win on the 7 shallow problems would hinge on crippling the forced_textual baseline (low N / sparse / decimal-h); to be a fair test, forced_textual's sampler must be pinned and pre-registered. geom_02 is the one problem where even a moderately dense numeric baseline still misses, so it should be analyzed separately.
